name: 西宁
description: Xining, capital of Qinghai Province, sits at 2,265m elevation and serves as the gateway to the Tibetan Plateau. Home to the grand Taiping Temple (Kumbum Monastery), stunning Qinghai Lake, and rich Tibetan and Hui ethnic cultures. A fascinating blend of ancient Buddhist monasteries, Islamic cuisine, and alpine scenery.
highlights: Taiping Temple (Kumbum Monastery) - Tibetan Buddhist architecture
Qinghai Lake - Sapphire of the Plateau
Mount青藏高原 and scenic drives

climate.tips: Best visited May-Oct for pleasant weather. Summers are mild and perfect for Qinghai Lake. High altitude - acclimatize 1-2 days. UV radiation is intense year-round. Winters cold but clear.

attractions.name: 塔尔寺
attractions.description: Historic Tibetan Buddhist monastery founded in 1583, featuring magnificent golden roofs, 930 halls, and the iconic pagoda built for the religion's founder's reincarnation.
attractions.tips: Arrive early to avoid tour groups. Respect religious practices. Watch for hourly prayers. The butter sculptures are legendary.

attractions.name: 青海湖
attractions.description: China's largest saltwater lake at 4,400m elevation, with stunning blue waters, golden rapeseed flowers, and habitat for endangered Tibetan antelope.
attractions.tips: Best May-Oct for flowers and weather. Stay overnight for sunrise. The 360km loop is spectacular by car or bike. Altitude sickness possible.

attractions.name: 青海省博物馆
attractions.description: Museum showcasing Qinghai's ethnic cultures, Tibetan artifacts, ancient Silk Road relics, and natural history exhibits.
attractions.tips: Good introduction before exploring the region. Audio guides available. Combine with nearby Xianfeng Temple.

attractions.name: 东关清真大寺
attractions.description: Historic mosque with stunning Chinese-Islamic architecture, one of the largest mosques in China serving the local Hui Muslim community.
attractions.tips: Modest dress required. Non-Muslims welcome outside prayer times. Friday prayers are spectacular. The nearby Muslim food street is excellent.

attractions.name: 日月山
attractions.description: Sacred mountain pass at 3,520m dividing agricultural and pastoral zones, with legendary princess story from Tang Dynasty.
attractions.tips: Stop on way to Qinghai Lake. High altitude - take it slow. The Tibetan legend is fascinating. Great photo opportunities.

attractions.name: 茶卡盐湖
attractions.description: Stunning salt flats known as 'Mirror of the Sky' where reflections create perfect mirror images on still water days.
attractions.tips: Best on calm, windless days. Arrive early for mirror effect. Wear colorful clothing for photos. Take off shoes for salt walking.

attractions.name: 讨赖河
attractions.description: Scenic river valley with canyon scenery, suspension bridges, and hiking trails through the Qilian Mountains.
attractions.tips: Good day trip from Xining. Beautiful in autumn. The river turns various colors. Popular picnic spot for locals.

attractions.name: 北山国家森林公园
attractions.description: Alpine forest park with waterfalls, streams, and peaks offering hiking and scenic plateau nature experiences.
attractions.tips: Great for nature lovers. Summer is best for waterfalls. Wear hiking shoes. Bring layers for changing weather.

attractions.name: 先锋伊斯兰艺术中心
attractions.description: Historic Hui Muslim mosque with unique architecture blending Chinese and Islamic elements.
attractions.tips: Smaller and quieter than Dongguan Mosque. Good for photography. The neighborhood has good Muslim restaurants.

attractions.name: 青海高原野生动物园
attractions.description: Zoo specializing in plateau wildlife including yaks, Tibetan antelope, and rare birds native to the high altitude regions.
attractions.tips: Great for families. The Tibetan antelope is endangered species. Morning visits for animal activity. Combine with nearby Beishan hiking.

attractions.name: 金银滩草原
attractions.description: Golden grassland famous as filming location for Chinese atomic bomb development and traditional Tibetan pastoral scenery.
attractions.tips: The secret atomic city museum is interesting. Stay overnight in yurt. Best for grassland scenery and cultural immersion.

attractions.name: 都兰海寺
attractions.description: Remote Tibetan Buddhist monastery on the plateau, less visited but offering authentic monastic experience and plateau scenery.
attractions.tips: Off the beaten path. Authentic experience without crowds. The drive is scenic. Bring snacks as facilities are limited.

attractions.name: 玉珠峰
attractions.description: Snow-capped 6,178m peak, sacred mountain and popular trekking base for climbers and hikers seeking alpine experiences.
attractions.tips: Only for experienced hikers at altitude. Best July-September. Acclimatize in Xining first. Hire local guide essential.

attractions.name: 黑马河乡
attractions.description: Small village on Qinghai Lake's west shore famous for sunrise views over the lake's eastern waters.
attractions.tips: Best sunrise location on the lake. Stay overnight in local inn. The lake at dawn is magical. Simple but authentic experience.

attractions.name: 鸟岛
attractions.description: Islands on Qinghai Lake that become breeding grounds for millions of migratory birds including bar-headed geese and black-necked cranes.
attractions.tips: Best in May-June for breeding season. Rest of year limited access. Bring binoculars. The bird spectacle is incredible.

attractions.name: 三股水
attractions.description: Sacred Tibetan spring with three water sources representing different blessings, surrounded by prayer flags and mountain scenery.
attractions.tips: Local Tibetans perform ritual washing. Respect religious practices. The mountain setting is beautiful. Combine with Kumbum visit.

attractions.name: 岗什卡雪峰
attractions.description: 5,254m snow-capped peak near the plateau, popular for climbing and offering stunning alpine scenery and plateau meadow walks.
attractions.tips: Good introduction to plateau mountaineering. July-September best. Base camp facilities available. Hire guide for summit attempt.

attractions.name: 坎尔斯森林
attractions.description: Lush forest area on the plateau, offering scenic hiking, camping, and autumn foliage viewing.
attractions.tips: Best in September-October for autumn colors. Camping permitted in designated areas. Popular with local hikers.

attractions.name: 湟源古镇
attractions.description: Historic town on the old Silk Road with traditional architecture, museums, and gateway to Tibetan plateau regions.
attractions.tips: Good stop between Xining and Qinghai Lake. The old street is charming. Try local barley wine. The architecture is well preserved.

attractions.name: 互助土族风情园
attractions.description: Cultural park showcasing the unique Tu ethnic minority traditions, dance, and folk customs with performances.
attractions.tips: Great cultural experience. The Nuoshufeng dance is famous. Try Tu barley wine. Combine with nearby mountains.

attractions.name: 阿尼玛卿雪山
attractions.description: Sacred Tibetan mountain range with glaciers and peaks, important religious site and trekking destination.
attractions.tips: One of Tibet's sacred mountains. Only for experienced trekkers. Altitude is extreme. Hire local Tibetan guide required.

attractions.name: 大通国家森林公园
attractions.description: Forest park with mountains, rivers, and summer retreats offering hiking and nature appreciation.
attractions.tips: Good summer escape. The forested mountains are refreshing. Popular with locals. Fall colors are beautiful.

restaurants.name: 青海名吃
restaurants.description: Popular restaurant serving authentic Qinghai cuisine including lamb dishes and plateau vegetables.

restaurants.name: 马忠食府
restaurants.description: Famous Muslim restaurant known for beef noodles and lamb skewers, a local institution for breakfast and meals.

restaurants.name: 青海穆斯林美食
restaurants.description: Night market serving Hui Muslim street food including kebabs, hand-pulled noodles, and sweet pastries.

restaurants.name: 雅君羊羔肉
restaurants.description: Renowned lamb restaurant specializing in baby lamb preparations, tender and flavorful plateau lamb.

restaurants.name: 土族风情美食
restaurants.description: Restaurant serving Tu ethnic cuisine including barley wine, potato dishes, and traditional Tu foods.

restaurants.name: 藏家乐
restaurants.description: Tibetan family restaurant near the monastery serving yak butter tea, tsampa, and Tibetan noodles.

restaurants.name: 手抓羊肉
restaurants.description: Traditional boiled lamb served with hands, the signature Qinghai grassland dish, tender and juicy.

restaurants.name: 互助青稞酒
restaurants.description: Breweries and restaurants serving local highland barley wine (qingkejiu), a must-try Qinghai specialty.

restaurants.name: 面片大王
restaurants.description: Popular noodle shop serving hand-pulled noodles in various styles, local breakfast institution.

restaurants.name: 青海湖鱼馆
restaurants.description: Restaurant serving fresh fish from Qinghai Lake, including golden line fish and carp in various preparations.

restaurants.name: 酥油茶馆
restaurants.description: Traditional Tibetan tea house serving yak butter tea, sweet tea, and tsampa, authentic local experience.

restaurants.name: 酿皮
restaurants.description: Famous Qinghai street food - rice bran skin with various toppings, refreshing and flavorful snack.

restaurants.name: 酸奶
restaurants.description: Fresh yak milk yogurt, thick and creamy, sold at streetside stalls and restaurants throughout Xining.

restaurants.name: 虫草炖鸡
restaurants.description: Premium restaurant serving expensive but authentic caterpillar fungus (cordyceps) and chicken soup, plateau delicacy.

restaurants.name: 沙柳路夜市
restaurants.description: Bustling night market with dozens of food stalls serving local and international cuisine.

restaurants.name: 马贵羊肉
restaurants.description: Legendary lamb restaurant with secret family recipe, famous throughout Qinghai for exceptional lamb dishes.

restaurants.name: 互助小吃
restaurants.description: Local snack shop serving Tu ethnic foods including potato dumplings and barley pastries.

restaurants.name: 茶卡盐湖餐厅
restaurants.description: Restaurant at salt lake serving local cuisine with salt-themed dishes and plateau specialties.

restaurants.name: 老字号烤肉
restaurants.description: Established BBQ restaurant with decades of history, serving quality lamb and beef skewers.

restaurants.name: 酥油花
restaurants.description: Cafe near monastery serving butter tea and simple meals, named after famous Kumbum butter sculptures.

restaurants.name: 清真手抓饭
restaurants.description: Popular Muslim restaurant serving rice with lamb toppings, carrots, and raisins, flavorful one-bowl meal.

restaurants.name: 枸杞奶茶
restaurants.description: Local milk tea with goji berries, unique Qinghai drink combining dairy with plateau goji.

restaurants.name: 青稞面
restaurants.description: Traditional Tibetan noodles made from highland barley, served with various toppings and soups.

restaurants.name: 果洛火锅
restaurants.description: Hotpot restaurant using high-quality plateau ingredients including yak meat, wild mushrooms, and herbs.

restaurants.name: 街边小吃摊
restaurants.description: Various street food stalls throughout Xining serving local snacks, fruits, and quick bites.

arrival.tips: Airport 30km from city. Airport bus (25 CNY) or taxi (80 CNY) to city. Good connections to Beijing, Shanghai, Chengdu.
Train journey to Qinghai Lake area is scenic. Sleeper berths recommended. Kumbum Monastery near station.

rental: Driver with car is best option

hotels.name: 青海宾馆
hotels.bookingTips: Best luxury option in city. Good base for sightseeing. Book ahead during peak season.

hotels.name: 西宁万达嘉华酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Newer property with western amenities. Good for first-time visitors. Book weekend rates.

hotels.name: 塔尔寺迎宾楼
hotels.bookingTips: Stay near monastery for early morning visit. Tibetan-style rooms. Simple but authentic.

hotels.name: 青海湖宾馆
hotels.bookingTips: Best for sunrise over lake. Book yurt stay for authentic experience. Advance booking essential in summer.

hotels.name: 互助土族青年旅舍
hotels.bookingTips: Great for cultural immersion. Tu ethnic homestay available. English limited but experience is authentic.

hotels.name: 茶卡盐湖民宿
hotels.bookingTips: Stay overnight for sunrise photos. Simple but clean accommodation. Great for photographers.

hotels.name: 西宁中心智选假日酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best western chain option. Good for English speakers. Consistent quality. Book direct for best rates.

hotels.name: 金银滩草原营地
hotels.bookingTips: Unique grassland experience. Stay overnight for sunset and stars. Bring warm clothes even in summer.

hotels.name: 西宁锦江之星
hotels.bookingTips: Reliable budget option. Multiple locations. Book online for discounts. Simple but adequate.

hotels.name: 阿尼玛卿雪山营地
hotels.bookingTips: For serious trekkers only. Base for Anyemaqen expeditions. Altitude is extreme. Guide required.

hotels.name: 海晏宾馆
hotels.bookingTips: Good base for western lake exploration. Quieter than main lake area. Simple local hotels.

hotels.name: 共和温泉宾馆
hotels.bookingTips: Perfect after long drives on plateau. Soothe muscles in hot springs. Great winter destination.

hotels.name: 祁连山草原民宿
hotels.bookingTips: Stay with local Tibetan or Mongolian families. Extremely authentic. Limited English. Bring gifts.

hotels.name: 门源油菜花民宿
hotels.bookingTips: Best July for flower season. The yellow flower fields are spectacular. Book well ahead in peak season.

hotels.name: 果洛藏饰客栈
hotels.bookingTips: For adventurous travelers only. Deep Tibetan plateau experience. Very limited English. Acclimatize first.

tips: Remote areas may not accept mobile

culturalTips.title: Tibetan Buddhist Respect
culturalTips.content: When visiting Kumbum Monastery, walk clockwise around pagodas, spin prayer wheels clockwise, and never photograph people praying without permission.

culturalTips.title: Islamic Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Dongguan Mosque is an active place of worship. Dress modestly, remove shoes before entering prayer halls, and avoid visiting during prayer times.

culturalTips.title: Qinghai Cuisine
culturalTips.content: Qinghai cuisine combines Tibetan, Mongolian, and Hui influences. Must-try: hand-grabbed lamb, yak butter tea, barley wine, and fresh yogurt.

culturalTips.title: Altitude Sickness Prevention
culturalTips.content: Xining is at 2,265m. Acclimatize 1-2 days before visiting higher areas like Qinghai Lake (3,200m). Drink plenty of water and avoid strenuous activity initially.

culturalTips.title: Minority Cultures
culturalTips.content: Qinghai has Tibetan, Hui, Tu, Mongolian, and Salar minorities. Each has unique customs and traditions. Respect local practices and ask before photographing.

culturalTips.title: Monastery Protocol
culturalTips.content: At Tibetan monasteries: don't step on thresholds, point feet away from statues, walk clockwise, and speak softly. Offer small donations if invited.

culturalTips.title: Tea House Culture
culturalTips.content: In Tibetan tea houses, receive tea with both hands. It's polite to keep your cup full - host will refill automatically. The butter tea is an acquired taste.

culturalTips.title: Plateau Environment
culturalTips.content: The plateau ecosystem is fragile. Stay on marked trails, don't disturb wildlife, carry out trash, and support sustainable tourism.

culturalTips.title: Alcohol Traditions
culturalTips.content: Barley wine (qingkejiu) is important in local culture. When toasting, hold cup with both hands and accept with both hands. It's polite to drink at least a sip.

culturalTips.title: Prayer Flags
culturalTips.content: Colorful prayer flags are sacred - don't step on them or remove them. When old, they should be replaced by burning, not thrown away.

culturalTips.title: Plateau Climate
culturalTips.content: Plateau weather changes quickly. Even summer nights are cold. UV radiation is intense year-round. Bring layers and sunscreen regardless of season.

culturalTips.title: Nomadic Communities
culturalTips.content: Some Tibetan and Mongolian families still practice traditional nomadic life. When visiting, ask permission and respect their way of life.

culturalTips.title: Local Products
culturalTips.content: Qinghai is famous for caterpillar fungus (cordyceps), goji berries, and Tibetan handicrafts. Buy from reputable shops and research prices beforehand.

culturalTips.title: Language Diversity
culturalTips.content: Mandarin is universal, but Tibetan, Mongolian, and Hui languages are also spoken. In remote areas, hand signals and patience help communication.

culturalTips.title: Gift Giving
culturalTips.content: When visiting Tibetan or Mongolian families, bring small gifts (candy, fruit, tea). Gifts are received with both hands. Avoid giving sharp objects like knives.

emergencyContacts.name: 青海省人民医院
emergencyContacts.notes: Best hospital in province, some English

emergencyContacts.name: 尼泊尔驻拉萨总领事馆
emergencyContacts.notes: For Nepal citizens in Qinghai region

emergencyContacts.name: 青海气象服务
emergencyContacts.notes: For plateau weather updates and forecasts

emergencyContacts.name: 青海森林防火
emergencyContacts.notes: Forest fire reporting and plateau emergencies

emergencyContacts.name: 青海路政
emergencyContacts.notes: For plateau road conditions and emergencies

quickFacts.water: Tap water safe after boiling; bottled water recommended
